violations.text: Cooked shrimp, and salted mackerel were observed held at 47degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Moved mackerel to a 41 deg F Refrigerator, and disposed of cooked shrimp. THe produce refrigerator is not to be used for any items that require refrigeration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2025

A sink from the poultry room is draining into a sink in the basement. All wastewater must be plumbed directly into a proper drain. PIC states that this is a temporary fix until the permanent pipe is repaired. Send pictures of repair to inspector at address provided. New Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2025

Tripe was observed thawing in the back room. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/12/2023

Inspector observed several thermometers in poor repair. Specifically in the meat display cases, the thermometers are not reading an accurate temperature and need replacement Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/20/2023

The dairy display refrigerator in the retail area was not in good repair. Specifically, the cooling unit on the right side of the refrigerated space was not working properly resulting in higher than normal temps in that area of the fridge (45 deg F). Product (milk, cheese) was moved to the left side of the unit (35 deg F) until the unit can be repaired. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/12/2023

The tile floor in the poultry area is in poor repair. Specifically, the grout in between the tiles is worn away, allowing po0ling of water in between the tiles on the floor. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2023
